Test: Verify that, authenticate API req res is successful
Description: Send a request to the authentication API with valid credentials.
Expected: Successful response (HTTP 200/201) and retrieve the access token.

Test: Verify that, create-wallet API req res retrieves a successful response
Description: Send a request to the wallet creation API.
Expected: Validate the response for expected fields (wallet token ID, creation timestamp).

Test: Verify that, a new card token can be added to a Wallet via API
Description:
Send a request to the tokenization API with valid card details.

Validate that the response contains a valid token and expected fields.
Expected: Verify that the API response confirms successful addition (HTTP 200/201)

Test: Verify that executing a query from the Wallet DB will retrieve the Wallet and Card token logs
Expected: Query the wallet database to confirm a new entry with the correct user ID, wallet ID, and token details.

Test: Verify that, card number, expiration date, CVV, zip/postal code,  input validations errors are returned via API
Description: Send requests with various invalid card numbers (incomplete, non-numeric, too short/long)
Expected: Validate that the API returns appropriate error messages and status codes (HTTP 400)

Test: Verify that, create-card token API returns a confirmation response after successfully adding a new card
Expected:
Ensure the response includes a success message, card token, wallet ID, and timestamps

Check the Wallet DB to confirm the card/token entry exists

Test: Verify that the card token generation  is logged in the DB
Expected: Query the wallet database to confirm a new entry with the correct user ID, wallet ID, and token details.

Test: Validate Wallet Tokenize Transaction - TokenId Stored in Wallet DB
Description:
• A valid request to add a token to the wallet is made with the necessary authentication and data.
  • Wallet DB is accessible for querying and contains the required schema for storing tokenized data.
Expected:
• The resultant tokenId is correctly generated and stored in the Wallet DB.
  • The tokenId can be queried and found in the Wallet DB.
  • API response: HTTP 200 OK (or equivalent success response indicating successful tokenization and storage).
